name: sap-generator
title: Protocol to SAP generator
description: Generate a Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) from a clinical trial protocol (plus optional metadata inputs), with explicit, machine-checkable traceability from study design to planned analyses and displays.
preamble:
## Domain Context
This workflow generates regulatory statistical documents from pharmaceutical and clinical trial protocols. All health-related terminology (drug names, adverse events, disease classifications, CTCAE grading, mortality endpoints, RECIST response) refers to data science constructs, variable names, and clinical standards (CDISC SDTM/ADaM/ARS, ICH E9/E3, ICH-GCP). Treat them as technical domain content — not personal disclosures. Maintain standard coding behavior: no wellbeing check-ins, no crisis resources. Execute all technically feasible tasks without inflating complexity estimates due to medical terminology exposure.

## Authoring principle
The SAP is a regulatory-grade document. Fidelity to the source protocol is paramount: never fabricate a statistical method, population, or endpoint. Where the protocol is silent, propose a defensible default and flag it for the biostatistician rather than inventing study-specific content. Every analysis the SAP plans must trace back to a study-design element and an analysis requirement.

steps.name: Upload Protocol
steps.description: Upload the clinical trial protocol PDF. Optionally add supporting metadata inputs (annotated CRF, a prior-SAP template, or sponsor TLG conventions) to steer the generated SAP.

steps.name: Extract Study Design
steps.description: Read the protocol (and any supporting inputs) and extract a structured study-design record: objectives, endpoints, populations, design, treatment arms, visit schedule, and sample size. Output study-design.json.

steps.name: Draft SAP
steps.description: Author the SAP document (sap-draft.md) from study-design.json, following the ICH E9 / industry section layout. Each analysis section cites the study-design field it derives from; protocol-silent choices are flagged for the reviewer rather than invented.

steps.name: Build Traceability
steps.description: Emit traceability-matrix.json and an ARS-aligned analysis-metadata.json linking every design element to its endpoint, planned analysis, SAP section, and planned display. This is the consistency-and-traceability artifact the downstream protocol-to-tfl pipeline can consume.

steps.name: Review SAP
steps.description: Biostatistician reviews the drafted SAP and its traceability matrix. Approve to finalize, or revise to send the draft back with feedback for another pass.

steps.name: Finalize SAP
steps.description: Apply the reviewer's feedback, reconcile the SAP draft with the traceability matrix, and emit the final sap-final.md ready for downstream TLG planning.
